claimText: The Global Drought Outlook, 2025 is released by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
evidenceExcerpt: Global Drought Outlook, 2025 Released by: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).
examUse: Statement-based question on reports and publishing organizations.

claimText: The Disaster Risk Index (DRI) was formally adopted by the 15th Finance Commission to guide disaster funding and distribute National Disaster Response Fund (NDRF) allocations.
evidenceExcerpt: Genesis: 15th Finance Commission (2021–26) formally adopted it to guide disaster funding. It integrated state’s DRI score into a formula for distributing NDRF and state contingency funds.
examUse: Statement-based question on the criteria used by the Finance Commission for disaster funding.

claimText: The composite score of the Disaster Risk Index (DRI) is calculated using the probability of hazards, which carries a weightage of 70, and vulnerability, which carries a weightage of 30.
evidenceExcerpt: Components: DRI uses probability of hazards (score of 70) and vulnerability (score of 30) to arrive at a composite score.
examUse: Statement-based question on the methodology and weightage of the Disaster Risk Index.
distractorHooks: Equal weightage for hazard probability and vulnerability
Vulnerability has a higher weightage than hazard probability
Includes capacity as a third component

claimText: The District Flood Severity Index, developed by researchers from IIT Delhi and Gandhinagar, quantifies flood severity using variables representing the occurrence of floods and the damage caused by them.
evidenceExcerpt: District Flood Severity Index Developed by: Researchers from IIT Delhi and Gandhinagar... Consists of six factors classified in two groups: Variables representing occurrence of floods... Variables representing damage due to floods.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on indices developed by Indian institutions.
distractorHooks: Only considers flood occurrence, not damage
